_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:19:52Z)**_

This is a static React mockup file under `docs/vercel_mockup/`. The data (`bookedRows`, `boardedRows`) is hardcoded inline literals, the "Export CSV" button has no `onClick` handler (no actual export happens), there is no backend, no auth context, no user-controlled input reaching any sink. The `vesselFilter` prop is compared via `===` and `Array.prototype.includes` (no injection surface), and `readOnly` only hides a button that does nothing.

Per the instructions ("Do NOT report: ... test/mock files"), and because there is no source→sink path with security impact in this mockup change, there are no findings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 16:19:56Z)**_

No security findings. This is a static mockup file (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`) with hardcoded data. The new `vesselFilter` prop uses safe equality/`includes` comparisons against literal vessel names, and the `readOnly` prop only conditionally renders a CSV button that has no `onClick` handler (no actual export logic). No entry points, no sinks, no attacker-controllable data flow.
